result.title: fix(account): Plan upgrade crashes on mobile viewports
result.summary:
Users on mobile devices get a "Checkout failed" error when trying to upgrade or switch their HogFlix subscription plan, while the same flow works fine on desktop.

**What's happening:** Error tracking shows a spike of `"Checkout failed: unable to process plan change"` exceptions originating from the Account page (`/account`). All affected sessions have viewport widths below 640px (mobile). The error fires immediately when tapping any plan upgrade button. Desktop users are unaffected.

**Root cause:** In `app/account/page.tsx`, the plan change button handler calculates an animation duration using `300 / (scale - 1)` where `scale = window.innerWidth / rect.width`. On desktop, the plan cards sit in a 3-column CSS grid (`sm:grid-cols-3`), so each card is roughly 1/3 of the viewport — `scale` is around 3 and the math works. On mobile (below the `sm` breakpoint), the grid collapses to a single column, so each card spans the full viewport width. This makes `rect.width === window.innerWidth`, therefore `scale === 1`, and `300 / (1 - 1)` produces `Infinity`. The subsequent `isFinite()` check then throws the error.

**How to resolve:** Remove the animation duration calculation entirely — it serves no purpose since no actual animation is implemented. Replace the onClick handler with a direct `updatePlan(p.id)` call. Alternatively, guard against `scale === 1` by defaulting to a fixed duration.

relevant_code_paths: app/account/page.tsx (line 118-130: onClick handler with the broken scale calculation)
app/account/page.tsx (line 90: data-plan attribute used by querySelector)

relevant_commit_hashes.198632a: Added the animation duration calculation to the plan upgrade flow that breaks on mobile viewports

findings.data_queried: Error tracking shows 47 occurrences of 'Checkout failed: unable to process plan change' in the last 7 days. 100% of affected sessions have $screen_width < 640px. Stack trace points to app/account/page.tsx onClick handler. The error is deterministic on mobile — every tap on a plan upgrade button triggers it.

relevant_code_paths: app/account/page.tsx (line 125: `const duration = 300 / (scale - 1)` — division by zero when scale===1)
findings.data_queried: Confirmed by inspecting the CSS: the `.grid.sm\:grid-cols-3` class uses Tailwind's responsive prefix, meaning below 640px the grid is single-column. Verified that on single-column layout, each plan card's width equals the container width (minus padding), which approximately equals window.innerWidth. The division by zero is therefore guaranteed on any mobile viewport.

relevant_commit_hashes.198632a: The commit that added the buggy handler

findings.data_queried: Session recordings show mobile users navigating to /account, scrolling to the plan section, tapping 'Upgrade', and seeing nothing happen (the error is thrown but not displayed to the user). Some users tap multiple times before giving up.

actionability.explanation: The fix is trivial: the entire animation duration calculation is dead code — no animation actually uses it. The onClick handler should just call `updatePlan(p.id)` directly, which is what the original code did before the regression. This is a one-line simplification that completely fixes the mobile checkout flow.

priority.explanation: Plan upgrades are completely broken for all mobile users. This is a revenue-blocking bug — users literally cannot change their subscription on mobile. The error is deterministic and affects 100% of mobile upgrade attempts. Trivial fix with zero risk.
